attacking_style: Opportunistic rather than explosive; attacks from breakaways on steep finales (Vuelta 2022) or bridges solo on penultimate climbs (Flèche Wallonne 2020). Rarely initiates attacks on final climbs, preferring to follow wheels then finish strongly.
team_tactics: EF Education team shows recurring positioning failures: multiple crashes within 5km (2018 Tour), slow bike changes losing 3 minutes (2018 Tour), and echelon gaps (2019 Tour). When executing, uses tempo control but Uran conserves energy rather than contributing to pace.
terrain_preference: Steady-gradient climbs suit his diesel motor. Exposed by irregular profiles with steep kicks then flat sections (Col de la Loze 2020). Time trial competent (top-10 2021 Tour) but not a strength versus pure climbers.
vulnerability: Team positioning failures leave him exposed to crashes and echelons; lost 2+ minutes in echelon stage 10 (2019 Tour), crashed from teammate wheel touch (2019 Vuelta). Cannot respond to explosive accelerations on irregular gradients with sudden steep sections.
key_patterns: Minimal work contribution during tempo phases, conserving energy for final efforts (2021 Tour, 2017 Tirreno)
Vulnerable to team positioning errors: crashes, slow bike swaps, echelon gaps cost 2-3 minutes repeatedly
Late-race resilience: halved GC deficit Vuelta 2022 at age 35, consistent podium 2020 Tour
Crumbles on irregular gradients with steep kicks, thrives on steady tempo climbs (2020 Tour evidence)
Tactical composure under adversity: won with broken derailleur 2017, solo bridge 2020 Flèche Wallonne
form_trajectory: positive: At age 35 in 2022 Vuelta, won from breakaway and halved GC deficit to reach top-10, demonstrating late-career resurgence after 2019-2021 decline marked by echelon gaps and podium losses
built_from: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 17
2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 17
